prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Reference DOM (React phone dial code only): react-phone-number-input style: separate dial code combobox and national number Reference DOM (Definition list form): Fields structured as dl/dt/dd definition list pairs Reference DOM (Ashby yes no buttons): Ashby-inspired yes/no as two styled buttons instead of native radio Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: duplicate-name-attrs, custom-combobox, decoy-form, iframe-apply, glued-label-text. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: unstyled div soup with inline styles only. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: React phone dial code only; Definition list form; Ashby yes no buttons.
